title: Lord Nyax
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Youmay|the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] legend|[[Irek Ismaren]], who also adopted this name}}
{{Deity

{{Quote|In ancient times, on Corellia, Lord Nyax was what parents threatened their children with if they didn't eat their stewfruit or go to bed on time.|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="ELRS" />}}
The legend of '''Lord Nyax''' was a part of the mythology of [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. A scary bedtime story was told to [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Youngling/Legends|children]] that [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Nyax came to steal young [[Gender/Legends|boys]] and [[Gender/Legends|girls]] from their [[Sleeper (furniture)|beds]]. Later in history, the folk tale of Lord Nyax was merged with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] covert missions to kidnap [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] younglings, and spread from Corellia over the wider [[galactic community]].

Throughout history, other beings had their identity assimilated to that of the evil spirit. At the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a Corellian sect known as the [[Cult of Lord Nyax]] carried out horrendous acts of [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorism]] against the local population in the name of their [[Deity/Legends|deity]]. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|Yuuzhan Vong invasion]], the [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] [[Irek Ismaren]] assumed the identity of Lord Nyax after hearing it in the minds of those he [[Torture/Legends|tortured]].

==History==
{{Quote|So our so-called Lord Nyax is a three-meter human male, possibly a Jedi, certainly a Force user, wandering around in a world where it probably doesn't understand anything.|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="ELRS" />}}
In times that were considered ancient by [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], the evil spirit known as [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] Nyax was referred to by [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Parent/Legends|parents]] to frighten [[Youngling/Legends|children]] into compliant behavior. Adults typically used phrases such as "If you keep on being a bad boy, Lord Nyax will come for you." He was described either as a monstrous pale [[Force ghost/Legends|ghost]]<ref name="ELRS" /> or as a man shrouded in a dark [[Cloak/Legends|cloak]], with sickly [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], a hooked [[Nose/Legends|nose]], sharp [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], and long nails.<ref name="Lord Nyax card" /> The latter depiction shared many similarities with the appearance of the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]].<ref name="SW VD">''[[Star Wars: The Visual Dictionary]]''</ref> and, more generally speaking, with the cosmetic effects of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] corruption.<ref name="KOTOR2">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''</ref><ref name="SWDP">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref>

[[File:Nyax_Cultist.jpg|thumb|left|Nyax cultists were ready to pay any price in service of their leader.]]
[[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Shortly after]] the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the Lord Nyax story became associated with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], who also appeared, seemingly at random, to steal and [[Death/Legends|kill]] [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] children in the dead of night before they could grow to become [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and a threat to the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. Following this assimilation, the Lord Nyax legend spread from Corellia all over [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the early [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the New Order.<ref name="ELRS" />

At the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], several Corellian organizations honored Lord Nyax as their elected [[Deity/Legends|deity]]. By [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], a group called the [[Cult of Lord Nyax]] had formed, that worshiped Nyax. The cultists donned dark [[Clothing/Legends|cloaks]] and caused a number of [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] attacks in his name. [[Lord Nyax (cult leader)|Their leader]] himself took up the name Lord Nyax, but was eventually killed by a [[Spacer/Legends|spacer]]. The [[Cult of Lord Nyax|Followers of Nyax]], another organization, also adored the evil ghost and promoted radical [[Anti-Jedi sentiment/Legends|anti-Jediism]]. When [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader traveled to Corellia to hunt down remaining Jedi, the Followers saw him as the reincarnation of their god.<ref name="SWG">{{GalaxiesAED}}</ref>

[[File:Lord_Nyax.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Irek Ismaren as "Lord Nyax"]]
In 27 ABY, the mentally-damaged [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]]-in-training [[Irek Ismaren]] assumed the name Lord Nyax, after reading it in the minds of his frightened victims. He understood this as a recognition of his status, as "Nyax" sounded unique, and "Lord" meant that he was more important than everything else. Under this new identity, Ismaren brought terror to the [[Coruscanti/Legends|citizens]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], after it [[Battle of Coruscant (Yuuzhan Vong War)|fell]] to the control of the [[Yuuzhan Vong]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Extragalactic/Legends|extra-galactic]] invaders. The fake Nyax was apparently [[Attack on the Sacred Spire|crushed to death]] by a combination of rocks and boulders from the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] ruins,<ref name="ELRS" /> though later reports hinted that he may in fact have survived.<ref name="JATM">''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Lord Nyax was first mentioned in ''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]'', a [[2002]] novel written by [[Aaron Allston]].<ref name="ELRS" /> In [[November]] [[2003]], the Cult of Lord Nyax appeared in the first expansion of the ''[[Star Wars]]''-themed [[Wikipedia:Massively multiplayer online role-playing game|MMORPG]] ''[[Star Wars Galaxies]]''. As Lord Nyax was a mythological character, he did not appear in ''Galaxies''. Nonetheless, the cult did have an imposter who went by the name Lord Nyax.<ref name="SWG" />

As a menacing figure towards children, Lord Nyax shares many similarities with the real-life legend of the [[Wikipedia:Bogeyman|bogeyman]], a type of make-believe figure that is sometimes used by parents to frighten their offspring into swift obedience.
